channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ex-NFLer Chris Kluwe says he was fired from job after MAGA protest
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe said he was fired from his high school football coaching position after a MAGA plaque protest.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian president thanks Trump, US on X day after heated meeting
item.description: (NewsNation) — The day after a contentious back-and-forth in the Oval Office, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy publicly thanked President Donald Trump and the U.S. for its support during the three-year war. "We are very grateful to the United States for all the support.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic activists fueled anti-Trump protests at GOP town halls but also rage at their own party
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Furious Democrats filled Republican town halls across America last week to protest President Donald Trump’s power grab in Washington.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, LISA MASCARO and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump reveals who he believes left infamous bag of cocaine at White House
item.description: President Donald Trump said in an interview this week he believes President Joe Biden or his son, Hunter Biden, left cocaine in the White House in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump endorses 'clean' CR as government shutdown looms
item.description: President Donald Trump supports idea of a "clean" continuing resolution as the prospect of a mid-March government shutdown looms. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Oval Office thrashing of Zelenskyy shows limits of Western allies' ability to sway US leader
item.description: President Donald Trump’s Oval Office thrashing of Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy has laid bare the limits of a full-court press by America’s allies aimed at reshaping Trump’s determination to end Russia’s invasion even if the terms are not to Ukraine’s liking.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: From Cabinet secretary to doomsday president: What being a designated survivor is like
item.description: The designated survivor for a president's address to Congress starts the day as a Cabinet secretary and ends it that way, too, God willing.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal workers start to get a new email demanding their accomplishments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal employees are starting to receive another email requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments, a renewed attempt by President Donald Trump and billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk to demand answers from the government workforce.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to an Oval Office meltdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first time he spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, President Donald Trump tried to pressure Ukraine's new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election. It was a 2019 phone call that eventually sparked an impeachment.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy leaves White House without signing minerals deal after Oval Office blowup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump berated Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for being “disrespectful” Friday in an extraordinary Oval Office meeting, then abruptly called off the signing of a minerals deal that Trump said would have moved Ukraine closer to ending its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European leaders support Ukraine amid tensions with Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — European leaders pledged late Friday to stand by Ukraine in the wake of the contentious Oval Office meeting between U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration approves major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has approved a major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel, bypassing a normal congressional review to provide the country with more of the 2,000-pound bombs that it has used in the war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What US lawmakers are saying about the White House clash between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key Republicans and Democrats in Congress have been stalwart supporters of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, but the blowup between the Ukrainian leader and President Donald Trump is threatening to change that.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US auto industry could be collateral damage in Trump's trade wars
item.description: President Donald Trump’s trade wars threaten to claim a casualty on the home front: the American auto industry.
item.dc:creator: Paul Wiseman And Alexa St. John, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Where Zelenskyy is headed following tense White House exchange with Trump, Vance
item.description: Ukraine President Vlodomyr Zelenskyy left the White House early on Friday after inking no minerals deal and holding a tense exchange with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SCOOP: GOP Ukraine supporters alarmed after explosive Trump, Zelenskyy meeting
item.description: Several House Republicans who are supportive of Ukraine are alarmed by the explosive White House meeting between Zelenskyy and Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch the full Trump-Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump shouted at Ukraine's leader on Friday during an extraordinary meeting in the Oval Office, berating President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for “gambling with millions of lives” and suggesting his actions could trigger World War III.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Those with gender dysphoria can no longer serve in military: Pentagon
item.description: (NewsNation) — A memo sent to Defense Department leaders Thursday states those with gender dysphoria are no longer eligible for military service. This document, written by Darin S.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's early backing of Ramaswamy for Ohio governor seen by some as meant to avoid a nasty primary
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The sun hadn't set on Republican Vivek Ramaswamy's gubernatorial campaign launch in Ohio earlier this week before President Donald Trump posted his endorsement of the Cincinnati-born biotech entrepreneur and former Department of Government Efficiency co-chair.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AG Pam Bondi demands 'full' Epstein files from FBI, vows investigation
item.description: The DOJ released hundreds of Epstein-related files Thursday to a small group of conservative influencers, but Bondi says there's still more to be uncovered.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: The Trump administration kills nearly all USAID programs
item.description: The Trump administration is terminating thousands of foreign assistance grants and awards, according to a court filing. The move effectively guts the six-decade-old agency.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis and Frank Langfitt, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrat proposes study on slavery's impact, possible reparations
item.description: A Nevada Democratic assemblymember is proposing a bill to create a commission to study discrimination against Black Americans and possible reparations.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
